commit.message:
fix(model-3d): fix face splitting — snap line to plane + use triangle lookup

Three fixes for face splitting:
1. Snap line endpoints onto face plane before 2D projection (fixes
   floating-point errors from matrixWorld.invert() causing missed
   intersections)
2. Use triangleToHEFace for O(1) face lookup instead of findFaceAtPoint
   (avoids point-in-polygon edge cases at vertices/edges)
3. Include updatedAt in edge geometry key so edges re-render after splits

feat(model-3d): HEMesh as single source of truth — edge rendering + kernel extrude

Replace EdgesGeometry (which shows triangulation diagonals) with topology-aware
edge rendering from HEMesh. Add kernel extrudeFace operation for SketchUp-style
push-pull directly on the half-edge mesh, bypassing the Manifold backend.

- Add extrudeFace() with 3 cases: face-on-solid, standalone, split face
- Wire push-pull to use kernel extrude when HEMesh is available
- Add heFaceId to FaceData for O(1) face lookup
- Update snap candidate generator to use HEMesh edges
- 62 kernel tests passing

commit.message:
feat: maximize A1 sheet consistency with deterministic SVG pipeline and demo cherry-pick mode

* feat: maximize A1 sheet consistency with deterministic SVG pipeline and demo cherry-pick mode

Enable the canonical baseline pipeline so 2D technical panels (floor plans,
elevations, sections) are rendered as deterministic SVG from the BuildingModel
instead of being sent to FLUX AI. This ensures perfect geometric consistency
across all technical drawings.

Key changes:
- Enable useCanonicalBaseline + strictDeterministic2D feature flags
- Harden CDS-to-BuildingModel fallback with defensive massing defaults
- Switch SVG theme to "artistic" for presentation-quality drawings
- Add background rectangles and fallback window generation to SVGs
- Remove FLUX.1-schnell, use FLUX.1-dev (40 steps) everywhere
- Front-load material hex colors in hero_3d/interior_3d prompts
- Strengthen hero control strength for axonometric and elevation panels
- Warm paper background (#FAF9F6) for A1 composition
- Add panel regeneration UI with per-panel "Regenerate" buttons
- Add "Recompose A1 Sheet" button for cherry-picking best panels
- Add tier diagnostic logging (SVG vs FLUX panel routing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add init_image fallback when Together.ai returns persistent 500 errors

When all 5 retry attempts fail with 500/503 and init_image was active
(geometry render or style reference), retry one final time WITHOUT
init_image. Together.ai sometimes rejects init_image payloads (large
SVGs, encoding issues) with 500 errors.

Also fixes:
- Float precision artifact on imageStrength (0.19999999999999996 → 0.2)
- Better error logging on first 500 to diagnose init_image issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: slots availability uses interval-based overlap, not menu duration

Root cause: slots API used durationMin (menu length, e.g. 60min) as the
overlap window for availability checks. A 60-min menu at 06:00 would
overlap with a 06:45 reservation (06:00+60=07:00 > 06:45), marking the
slot as full even though no reservation exists at 06:00.

Changes:
- Overlap window now uses slotIntervalMin (grid cell width, e.g. 45min)
  instead of menu durationMin. This keeps slots consistent with the
  admin ledger grid. Menu duration is validated at /reserve time.
- Per-staff query now includes unassigned (staff_id='any'/NULL)
  reservations in conflict check — prevents showing a staff as
  available when an unassigned reservation occupies that time.
- Loop boundary changed to t <= closeMs to match admin grid's
  generateTimeSlots(min <= closeMin).

Verified on production:
- default: 06:00 available, 06:45+ full (correct for 1 staff, 8 resv)
- store-aea0: matches reservation data exactly
- durationMin parameter no longer changes slot availability

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix missing trades: paginate Form 4 search + fix aggregation key

  request) so scan_limit > 100 is actually honoured; previously hard-
  capped at 100 regardless of config
- Fix aggregate_trades key: use transaction_type (P/S/M/G/F) instead of

* feat(antfarm): add telegram-text-router workflow for V1 intent routing

New Antfarm workflow (workflow #18) that routes incoming Telegram TEXT
messages to backend services based on two-stage intent classification.

5 agents, 5 steps:
  classify_intent -> read_plc_state -> dispatch_to_service ->
  format_response -> capture_to_brain

Routes:
  DIAGNOSE   -> POST /diagnose (diagnosis service with RAG)
  IO         -> format live PLC tags for display
  STATUS     -> health check all services
  TROUBLESHOOT -> TreeRunner guided sessions
  GENERAL    -> Gus conversational response

Includes brain-recorder agent that auto-captures diagnosis results
and troubleshoot resolutions to Mem0 for future KB retrieval.

Closes the V1 demo flow gap: text questions were classified but
had no backend routing (photos handled by wiring-telegram workflow).

commit.message:
Implement MVP document export system

DIAGNOSIS:
- Backend had working PDF generation (Puppeteer) but frontend was completely disconnected
- All export buttons were placeholders (just alert() calls)
- Users couldn't actually download any reports
- Two incompatible data models (old IssueAction vs new FullAssessment)

MVP SOLUTION - 3 Essential Outputs:
1. Assessment Report PDF - Full report with submission gate, issues, evidence, actions
2. Issues Tracker CSV - Sortable spreadsheet for project management tools
3. Engagement Brief PDF - Specialist briefs with scope and deliverables

IMPLEMENTATION:
Frontend:
- Created services/exportService.ts with all export functions
- Connected ResultsDashboard export handlers to real API calls
- Updated Results.tsx mobile export to use real service
- Removed all placeholder alert() calls

Backend:
- Created routes/export.ts with POST endpoints for PDF generation
- Implemented HTML generators for reports, summaries, and briefs
- Uses Puppeteer for PDF rendering with clean, professional styling
- Registered export router in main index.ts

TECHNICAL DECISIONS:
- MVP-first: Simple, useful, maintainable over ambitious and broken
- PDF via Puppeteer: Already in stack, fast to implement
- CSV client-side: Instant generation, universal format
- Deferred: DOCX track changes, PowerPoint, email integration (too complex for 1-week turnaround)

OPTIMIZATION:
- Focused on speed (assessment in minutes, export in seconds)
- Enables 1-week turnaround goal
- Outputs enable immediate decisions and actions
- No over-engineering

See EXPORT_MVP_IMPLEMENTATION.md for full documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove separate translation columns, move all translations inline as .tr spans

- K-II (Konjunktiv II) table: remove empty 3rd column, move form translations inline (wäre → я бы был, etc.)
- lassen table: remove Перевод column, move sentence translations inline after examples
- Reflexivverben table: remove Перевод column, convert plain Russian to .tr spans in verb column
- Partizipien table: remove Значение column, move descriptions inline in Образование column
- Союзы / Nebensätze table: remove Значение column, move meanings inline in Союз column

fix: guaranteed image delivery with AI→placehold.co fallback chain

When Pollinations is down (502/1033):
- Send Concept Photo URL fails → Build Concept Fallback Text
- New IF DRAW Fallback routes DRAW mode to Send Concept Fallback Photo
- Send Concept Fallback Photo sends styled placehold.co image (always works)
- TEXT mode still routes to Send Concept Text Fallback (text TZ)

Result: user ALWAYS gets an image in DRAW mode:
  Pollinations UP  → real AI image
  Pollinations DOWN → colored concept image (placehold.co with HEX colors)

Also: Build Design Concepts now exports styleEn/bg/fg fields for fallback use.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Version 0.1.0 — Shield Visuals, Cloaking Overhaul & Ship Repulsion

Shield renderer: truncated icosahedron mesh with animated hex panels,
procedural energy texture, per-panel hit flash and crack effects.

Cloaking system: chunk-level entity hiding (MixinChunkMap, MixinTrackedEntity),
external shimmer renderer, radar filter mixin, Sodium compatibility mixin,
combat break (3 hits from/into cloaked ship → auto-decloak + 30s cooldown).

Solid shield: replaced VSDistanceJoint with force-based AABB repulsion —
SAT minimum penetration axis, quadratic force scaling, zero torque (no tumbling).

OBJ models: Cloaking Field Generator, Resonance Beacon (forge:obj pipeline).
Aetheric Compass: new needle texture, updated 32-frame animation.
New textures: attuned void shard, calibrated oscillator.

Bug fixes: cloak break FROM detection (worldAABB containment instead of
getShipManagingPos), solid barrier rotation lock on contact, shield cloud
sorting in shader, compass direction calculation.

Documentation: CHANGELOG, README, Player Guide (EN/RU), Code Guide updated.

commit.message:
Fix UTF-8 encoding in Muse Claude client

Use :erlang.list_to_binary/1 instead of to_string/1 when converting
:httpc response body. The charlist contains raw UTF-8 bytes, and
to_string treated each byte as a Unicode codepoint, corrupting
multi-byte characters like em dashes (— → â).

fix: ACF block schema lookup + wysiwyg markdown conversion (J1/J2)

Root cause: custom_block() stripped the acf/ prefix before calling
get_block_schema(), so schema lookup always failed (bouton != acf/bouton).
This caused no markdown conversion (J1) and no field key injection (J2).

- Remove $short_name prefix stripping in custom_block()
- Add wysiwyg case in custom_block() for markdown→HTML conversion
- Add else branch in process_acf_fields() for non-null wysiwyg values
- Add AcfAdapterTest (5 tests) + AcfFieldsTest wysiwyg test (1 test)

commit.message:
feat: concurrency hardening — migration 030 + 7 code fixes (Sprint 005)

CRITICAL C-1: UNIQUE partial index on chat_auto_sequences (one active per chat)
HIGH H-1: Transaction + 23505 catch in startSequence() (TOCTOU race)
HIGH H-2: processing_locked_at row-level lock for cron sequence processor
MEDIUM M-2: Advisory lock per store in dialogue sync + optimistic locking
MEDIUM M-4: Transaction wrapper for complaint-statuses bulk updates
MEDIUM M-5: 5-second dedup guard in insertStatusHistory
LOW L-3: 5-minute time guard on stale awaiting_reply cleanup

Migration: 030_sequence_concurrency_hardening.sql
Docs: BACKEND-CONCURRENCY-AUDIT.md, TASK-docs-004-IMPL-PLAN.md

setDoc+merge:true con payload parcial podía dejar points/breakdown

fallar la comprobación points==resource.data.points.
updateDoc garantiza que request.resource.data incluye el documento
completo fusionado.

commit.message:
feat: Batch 12 discovery — STOCH Sharpe 2.55, highest return ever (+23.6%)

5 untried combos: CCI+WILLR, STOCH+MFI, MFI+RSI, STOCH+RSI, MFI+ROC.
STOCH+MFI winner (pure STOCH short) validated at Sharpe 2.55, +23.6% return.
GA prefers single-indicator strategies — 3/4 runs converged to pure indicator.
MFI+RSI failed DSR. MFI+ROC had 35% validation degradation (overfitting).
